rationale: All three review lenses ran successfully. All 8 spec acceptance criteria satisfied: group/group-hover/focus-within/transition-opacity applied exactly as specced, no structural changes, no test changes. Two deferred items noted for awareness but not blocking: (1) touch/mobile devices cannot trigger group-hover — button is permanently hidden for touch-only users; this is a known limitation of the user-approved hover-based solution, not a regression introduced by this change; (2) unnamed Tailwind group is the established pattern in 4 sibling components (PinnedRow, Card, WorkflowCard, PinnedAssistantsOverflowDropdown) and carries the same ancestor-hover leakage risk as those components. Three dismissed: DOMPurify guards injected HTML making group-class leakage theoretical; the overlay-on-hover position is pre-existing not regressed; ghost-click risk is ~150ms window for a non-destructive copy action. No blocking findings.

business_review.criterion: button container hidden at rest via `opacity-0`
button reveals on keyboard focus via `focus-within:opacity-100`
button remains in DOM and tab order (opacity-only, not display:none or visibility:hidden)
business_review.notes: Confirmed — opacity-0 preserves accessibility tree presence and tab order

business_review.criterion: no structural changes — only Tailwind class changes to two divs

business_review.notes: Spec states no test changes; existing 3 tests pass unchanged

standards_review.notes: Commit 'EPMCDME-13243: Hide copy button by default, show on hover/focus' matches ticket-id prefix convention

standards_review.standard: Tailwind-only styling (no new SCSS for component states)
standards_review.notes: Pure Tailwind utility class change, consistent with styling-guide

standards_review.standard: No new React state or hooks introduced
standards_review.notes: Pure CSS change, component remains a stateless functional component
